Blight.Name: Northern Corn Leaf Blight
Blight.Causes: Fungal disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum
Blight.Prevention: Use resistant hybrids, rotate crops, and remove crop debris
Blight.Treatment: Apply fungicides such as strobilurins or triazoles

Common_Rust.Name: Common Rust
Common_Rust.Causes: Fungal disease caused by Puccinia sorghi
Common_Rust.Prevention: Plant resistant varieties and avoid dense planting
Common_Rust.Treatment: Use fungicides (triazoles, strobilurins) during early infection

Gray_Leaf_Spot.Name: Cercospora Leaf Spot (Gray Leaf Spot)
Gray_Leaf_Spot.Causes: Fungal disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis
Gray_Leaf_Spot.Treatment: Apply triazole-based fungicides and improve air circulation

Healthy.Name: Healthy Corn Leaf
Healthy.Prevention: Maintain proper irrigation and nutrient management

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.Name: Bacterial Spot
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.Causes: Bacterial infection caused by Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.Prevention: Use certified seeds, avoid overhead watering, rotate crops
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.Treatment: Copper-based bactericides and removal of infected leaves

Tomato_Early_blight.Name: Early Blight
Tomato_Early_blight.Causes: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani
Tomato_Early_blight.Prevention: Crop rotation, avoid wet leaves, and use resistant varieties

Tomato_Late_blight.Name: Late Blight
Tomato_Late_blight.Prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds and avoid waterlogging
Tomato_Late_blight.Treatment: Apply fungicides (mancozeb, metalaxyl) and destroy infected plants

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.Name: Leaf Mold
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.Causes: Fungal disease caused by Passalora fulva
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.Prevention: Increase air circulation and avoid high humidity in greenhouses
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.Treatment: Use sulfur or copper fungicides and prune infected leaves

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.Name: Septoria Leaf Spot
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.Causes: Fungal disease caused by Septoria lycopersici
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.Prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation and practice crop rotation

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.Name: Two-Spotted Spider Mite Infestation
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.Prevention: Maintain proper humidity and avoid drought stress
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.Treatment: Use miticides or neem oil and remove heavily infested leaves

Tomato_healthy.Name: Healthy Tomato Leaf
Tomato_healthy.Prevention: Ensure proper sunlight, watering, and nutrition
